content.full_text:
July 22, 2008

Chad Crowe
Department of Personnel Administration – IMS
1515 S Street, North Bldg. Suite 400
Sacramento, California 95811

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-113

Dear Mr. Crowe:

This letter is in response to your request for information regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act.1 You are employed by the Department of Personnel Administration. You were previously employed by SRA International where you worked on a project at the Department of Justice ("DOJ"). The DOJ has requested that you return to work part-time for a subcontractor of SRA International to complete the DOJ project. You ask whether you are permitted to do so. There is nothing in the Act that prohibits you from holding these dual positions. However, there are other laws outside the Act, and which we do not address, that may apply, such as the Department of Personnel Administration’s incompatible activities policy or matters implicating Public Contract Code Section 10410. We, therefore, suggest you seek advice from your new agency’s counsel.

If you have questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5783.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Valentina Joyce
Counsel, Legal Division

1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014.
